medications.indication_rationale: First-line to heal oesophagitis and control symptomatic GORD; source used pantoprazole 40mg daily 30 min before breakfast for 4 weeks. Appropriate ONCE ACS is not the working concern and a cardiac safety-net is in place.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 40mg orally once daily, 30–60 min before breakfast, for 4 weeks (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Rapid symptomatic relief as an adjunct; source gave an antacid liquid with good relief.
medications.dose_route_frequency: per product directions PRN (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: PPI/antacid as a substitute for excluding ACS
medications.indication_rationale: Do NOT use symptomatic response to acid suppression as a way to 'rule out' cardiac pain — it is not diagnostic and delays ACS assessment. Treat GORD but keep the cardiac safety-net explicit.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid large late/fatty meals; no food or drink (except water) within ~3 hours of bed; reduce peppermint, chocolate, caffeine and alcohol.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: These relax the lower oesophageal sphincter or increase reflux; timing avoids nocturnal reflux.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Elevate the head of the bed / use a wedge to raise the whole torso.

patient_education_points.point: Your symptoms fit reflux — acid coming up after a big late meal, worse lying down, eased by antacids. A PPI plus not eating late and raising the head of the bed should settle it over a few weeks.

Important: chest pain can occasionally be the heart even when it feels like reflux, and I can't check your heart over chat. If you get chest tightness/pressure, pain spreading to the arm or jaw, sweating, breathlessness, or pain brought on by exertion — call 000 immediately. If in doubt about this episode, it's reasonable to have an ECG.
See a doctor promptly if you vomit blood or coffee-ground material, pass black tarry stools, have trouble swallowing, or lose weight — these need urgent assessment.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to PPI; consider H. pylori testing; endoscopy if alarm features/refractory; cardiac assessment if any doubt.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any cardiac features — 000; GI-bleed/dysphagia/weight loss — urgent GP/ED.

must_include_items: Screen ACS red flags and maintain an explicit cardiac safety-net (do not accept 'just reflux')
State that telehealth cannot exclude ACS; low threshold for ECG/in-person review if any doubt
acceptable_alternatives: GP follow-up at 4 weeks; H. pylori testing
errors_of_omission: No ACS red-flag screen / no cardiac safety-net
Not acknowledging telehealth cannot exclude ACS
errors_of_commission: Declaring it definitively non-cardiac / 'just reflux' and closing the encounter
Using antacid/PPI response as proof it isn't cardiac
Failing to advise 000 for cardiac features
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires BOTH GORD treatment AND an explicit ACS red-flag screen with a cardiac safety-net and acknowledgement that ACS cannot be excluded remotely. Confident non-cardiac reassurance without a cardiac safety-net is a serious failure.
